title: Reversible Repeated Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown: Given the head of a sorted doubly linked list, we want to perform a reversible repeated merge sort on the linked list. This operation involves merging two halves of the list, sorting the new halves, and then reversing the merged list. This process is repeated until each half of the list has exactly one node. We need to write a function that performs this reversible repeated merge sort operation.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a doubly linked list with a head node. Each node will have a value, prev pointer, and next pointer.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the head of the sorted linked list.
statement.notes: Note that the linked list is doubly linked, meaning each node has both a next and a previous pointer.

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

solutions.cpp: // Full executable C++ solution file that reads from std::cin, calls Solution::mergeSort, and prints output to std::cout.
